publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and runnable core task for Generic types and methods; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Generic types and methods: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Generic type arguments are fully reified at runtime, so new T() and new T[10] are always available.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use type parameters for compile-time relationships and account for type erasure; avoid raw types and require factories or reifiable representations when runtime construction is needed.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Use type parameters for compile-time relationships and account for type erasure; avoid raw types and require factories or reifiable representations when runtime construction is needed.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Use type parameters for compile-time relationships and account for type erasure; avoid raw types and require factories or reifiable representations when runtime construction is needed.
Generic type arguments are fully reified at runtime, so new T() and new T[10] are always available.

answer.explanation: Use type parameters for compile-time relationships and account for type erasure; avoid raw types and require factories or reifiable representations when runtime construction is needed.

items.title: Generic types and methods: runnable core task
items.prompt: Read whitespace-separated words. Store the first word in Box<String> and return it through the generic type without a cast.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Start from the stated input/output contract, implement the smallest language-specific operation, and run the visible boundary before changing structure.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden Java: Beginner to Intermediate runner checks match the declared contract.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic runner check differs; inspect input parsing, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Compiles or parses and passes every visible and hidden deterministic runner check for the declared contract.

privateAuthorNotes: Reference solution and both tests are AI-assisted draft material. Execute in the pinned runner and obtain independent human review before any exam use.

answer.explanation: The type parameter preserves the String relationship from construction to retrieval and avoids a raw type or cast.
